feed.title: Loop Library
feed.subtitle: Practical AI agent workflows for engineering, research, editorial work, evaluation, and operations.

entry.title: The docs sweep
entry.summary: A reusable AI coding-agent workflow for comparing documentation with the current codebase, fixing drift, and opening a reviewable pull request.

entry.title: The architecture satisfaction loop
entry.summary: A bounded refactoring workflow that live-tests the system, runs an independent review, commits checkpoints, and records progress.

entry.title: The sub-50 ms page-load loop
entry.summary: A performance optimization workflow for coding agents that uses one repeatable benchmark and stops only when every target page meets the threshold.

entry.title: The production error sweep
entry.summary: A scheduled production-log workflow that traces actionable errors to root causes, verifies fixes, opens a pull request, and stops cleanly when no action is needed.

entry.title: The 100% test coverage loop
entry.summary: A goal-based coding-agent workflow that identifies uncovered behavior, adds meaningful tests, and stops when the full suite passes at 100% coverage.

entry.title: The SEO/GEO visibility loop
entry.summary: A repeatable search visibility workflow that fixes the highest-impact crawl, indexation, page-intent, citation, and answer-readiness gaps first.

entry.title: The logging coverage loop
entry.summary: A goal-based observability workflow that audits important paths, adds useful structured logs, and verifies success and failure events with tests.

entry.title: The nightly changelog loop
entry.summary: A scheduled coding-agent workflow that reviews the previous day's changes and keeps user-facing release history complete and current.

entry.title: The quality streak loop
entry.summary: A realistic product-testing workflow that turns every failure into documented regression coverage and restarts the success streak after each fix.

entry.title: The full product evaluation loop
entry.summary: A comprehensive product-quality workflow that evaluates realistic scenarios across every major capability, fixes weak outcomes, and reruns them to the defined bar.

entry.title: The test-suite speed loop
entry.summary: A performance workflow for reducing test runtime under repeatable conditions without weakening coverage, assertions, isolation, or behavior.

entry.title: The repository cleanup loop
entry.summary: A repository-hygiene workflow that audits branches, pull requests, commits, and worktrees, recovers valuable changes, and removes proven stale state.

entry.title: The stale-safe batch release loop
entry.summary: A release-coordination workflow that excludes stale or unfinished work, combines valid changes, and ships complete artifacts from the latest integrated main.

entry.title: The production data cleanup loop
entry.summary: A production-data quality workflow that removes disallowed records, improves classification logic, and verifies the remaining dataset against an explicit definition.

entry.title: The post-release baseline loop
entry.summary: A triggered release workflow that runs standard benchmarks against the completed release and records a reproducible baseline for future comparisons.

entry.title: The ticket-to-PR-ready loop
entry.summary: A bounded engineering workflow that turns a ticket, failing behavior, or customer complaint into a proven root cause, minimal patch, and reviewer-ready handoff.

entry.title: The customer AI deployment loop
entry.summary: A supervised delivery workflow that advances one customer priority into a validated, gradually released AI system with monitoring, approvals, and outcome evidence.

entry.title: The product update podcast loop
entry.summary: A scheduled editorial workflow that turns meaningful public product changes into a short, source-grounded podcast episode.

entry.title: The Clodex adversarial-review loop
entry.summary: A Claude-and-Codex workflow that opens a pull request, runs an independent Codex review, fixes blocking findings, and repeats.

entry.title: The Loop Harness verification loop
entry.summary: A scheduled Loop Harness workflow that runs Claude in an isolated worktree and ships staged output only after a second Claude session verifies it.

entry.title: The Boeing 747 benchmark
entry.summary: A vision benchmark in which an agent builds a Boeing 747 from Three.js primitives, renders nine repeatable angles, and fixes what each view reveals.

entry.title: War Loops: frontend reconstruction
entry.summary: A War Loops workflow that captures a real page, builds a static Pencil mirror and moving Forge version, then repairs the weakest fidelity signals.

entry.title: The self-improving champion loop
entry.summary: A prompt-optimization workflow that tests challengers on a working set, promotes only fresh holdout wins, and keeps the current champion on uncertainty.

entry.title: The devil's-advocate loop
entry.summary: A critic-and-builder workflow that attacks a design, tracks every objection, and requires evidence before an objection can be closed.

entry.title: The fresh-clone loop
entry.summary: A disposable-environment workflow that follows the README from scratch, fixes every hidden setup assumption, and restarts until onboarding works cleanly.

entry.title: The Infinite Clickbait thumbnail loop
entry.summary: A thumbnail workflow that creates ten concepts, scores the top three against a relevant YouTube channel, and improves the winner without misleading viewers.

entry.title: The autonomy-loop builder-reviewer loop
entry.summary: An autonomy-loop workflow in which a builder and adversarial reviewer pass a git baton between worktrees and prove each new test can catch its fix.

entry.title: The Codex completion-contract loop
entry.summary: A goal-planner-codex workflow that defines completion up front, tracks proof for every requirement, and prevents partial Codex work from being reported as done.

entry.title: The Revolve versioned-experiment loop
entry.summary: A Revolve workflow that improves prompts, code, or configurations through checkpointed experiments whose scores remain comparable across sessions.

entry.title: The five-minute repository maintainer loop
entry.summary: A five-minute Codex workflow that triages repositories, directs bounded maintenance to dedicated threads, and requires proof and permission before work lands.

entry.title: The recent-feedback sweep
entry.summary: A project audit that turns recent user-reported problems into reusable failure patterns, fixes every confirmed match, and verifies a clean final sweep.

entry.title: The promise-to-proof loop
entry.summary: A product review that compares claims in marketing, documentation, demos, and AI answers with current evidence, then fixes or narrows unsupported promises.

entry.title: The propagation compliance loop
entry.summary: A consistency check for values copied across a code project: update every affected copy, find leftovers, and prove that only intentional old references remain.

entry.title: The multi-LLM convergence loop
entry.summary: Alternates two AI systems from different providers to review a plan, document, or code change until both approve the exact same version.

entry.title: The Goal Forge loop
entry.summary: A planning workflow that interviews the user, writes what should be built in SPEC.md, and writes how Codex should execute and verify it in GOAL.md.

entry.title: The UI/UX Score Loop
entry.summary: A browser-based review that completes a real user task, scores each meaningful screen with the same checklist, improves weak spots, and retests the whole task.

entry.title: The cold-load trimmer loop
entry.summary: A web performance workflow that reduces the data downloaded before the first screen appears, while tests and screenshots protect behavior and appearance.

entry.title: The pixel-safe CSS trim loop
entry.summary: A stylesheet cleanup workflow that removes one piece of unused or redundant CSS at a time and keeps it removed only when every tested screen looks identical.

entry.title: The easy onboarding loop
entry.summary: A first-time-user test that starts with no saved account or browser state, fixes one confirmed onboarding obstacle, and retries the entire experience.

entry.title: The accessibility repair loop
entry.summary: An accessibility review that confirms barriers against an agreed standard, fixes the issue with the greatest user impact, and repeats the same checks.

entry.title: The housekeeper loop
entry.summary: A conservative code-project cleanup that proves one small opportunity is safe, makes the smallest useful change, and keeps it only after existing checks pass.

entry.title: The Axelrod subagent arena loop
entry.summary: A controlled tournament where two reasoning AI agents repeatedly choose to cooperate or defect, then are compared with players that always make one choice.

entry.title: The prepare-a-new-project loop
entry.summary: A planning workflow that closes documentation gaps until requirements, technical design, acceptance criteria, and test strategy describe one buildable system.

entry.title: The test stabilizer loop
entry.summary: A flaky-test repair workflow that measures inconsistent results, fixes one root cause at a time, and stops after a defined streak of stable full-suite runs.

entry.title: The artifact-to-skill loop
entry.summary: A reusable workflow for turning one proven artifact into a transferable skill, playbook, or procedure and validating it on a second case.

entry.title: The Strip Miner loop
entry.summary: An evidence-driven workflow-mining loop that finds repeated successes in authorized coding-agent history, rejects contradicted candidates, and validates extracted loops with fresh replay.

entry.title: The Living Story loop
entry.summary: A recurring context-maintenance workflow that turns repository activity, goals, and prior open threads into a verified daily story for future agents.

entry.title: The Groundtruth loop
entry.summary: A read-only project-audit workflow that verifies architecture, security, platform behavior, operations, and business logic from current evidence rather than assumptions.

entry.title: The Recovery Proof loop
entry.summary: A disaster-recovery validation workflow that restores randomly selected real recovery points, verifies integrity and RPO/RTO, and preserves failures as regression drills.

entry.title: The refund follow-up loop
entry.summary: A persistent follow-up workflow that starts a refund claim, watches replies and deadlines, and keeps the case moving until the money arrives.
